name: Reverse Pec-Deck
resistance_profile: Horizontal abduction against a stable machine path; ideal for hard, safe rear-delt work.
rom_notes: Open the arms back through a full horizontal abduction; control the return to a stretch.
cues: Keep a soft elbow angle fixed
Squeeze the rear delts, control back
execution_steps: Adjust the seat so the handles sit at shoulder height, then sit facing the pad with your chest against it.
Grab the handles with your arms extended in front of you and a soft, fixed elbow bend.
Open your arms back in a wide arc, leading with your elbows and pinkies, until your arms are out to your sides.
Squeeze your rear delts hard, then control the handles back to a stretch in front of you.
good_when: You want to push rear delts hard and safely along a fixed, stable path.
You are a beginner who wants a near-foolproof rear-delt movement.
You want to isolate the rear delts with minimal balance or setup demand.
bad_when: No reverse pec-deck machine is available — use a cable or dumbbell version instead.
You want a stretch-loaded (lengthened) stimulus — this machine peaks at the contracted end.
common_errors: Short range at the contracted end
